tool_use.param: Run pytest on specified test files with verbose output

def test_json_success() -> None: """JSON output on successful dispatch includes all expected fields.""" runner = CliRunner() with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env: env.setup_repo_structure()
git = FakeGit( git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir}, default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},

catch_exceptions=False, assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Command failed: {result.output}" except json.JSONDecodeError: > raise AssertionError(f"No JSON line found in output:\n{output}") E AssertionError: No JSON line found in output: tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py:46: AssertionError tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py:46: AssertionError =============================== warnings summary =============================== /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py:16: DeprecationWarning: 'BaseCommand' is deprecated and will be removed in Click 9.0. Use 'Command' instead. -- Docs: https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/how-to/capture-warnings.html FAILED tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py::test_json_success - AssertionError: No JSON line found in output: FAILED tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py::test_json_dry_run - AssertionError: No JSON line found in output: FAILED tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py::test_json_error_empty_prompt - AssertionError: No JSON line found in output: "error_type": "invalid_input", FAILED tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py::test_json_error_invalid_repo - AssertionError: No JSON line found in output: "error_type": "invalid_repo", FAILED tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py::test_json_no_human_on_stdout - AssertionError: No JSON line found in output: FAILED tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py::test_json_stdin_input - AssertionError: No JSON line found in output: =================== 6 failed, 1 passed, 2 warnings in 1.87s ====================
session.assistant:
## Test Results
**Summary:** 1 passed, 6 failed
**Passing:** - `tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py::test_output_types_matches_return_annotation` - PASSED
**Failing:** All 6 tests in `tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py` failed with the same root cause:
1. **test_json_success** - FAILED 2. **test_json_dry_run** - FAILED 3. **test_json_error_empty_prompt** - FAILED 4. **test_json_error_invalid_repo** - FAILED 5. **test_json_no_human_on_stdout** - FAILED 6. **test_json_stdin_input** - FAILED
### Root Cause
The test helper `_extract_json()` function is looking for a **single-line JSON object** but the actual output has JSON formatted as **pretty-printed multi-line JSON** at the end of the output.
**Expected by test:** JSON on a single line containing key `"success"` **Actual output:** Pretty-printed multi-line JSON block: ```json { "dry_run": false, "pr_number": 1, "run_id": "run-1", "branch_name": "plnd/test-title-test-body-01-15-1430", "pr_url": "https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/1", "run_url": "https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/run-1", "success": true } ```
The test extraction function cannot parse multi-line JSON because it only checks individual lines, each of which is invalid JSON on its own (e.g., `{`, `"dry_run": false,`, `}`).
